title: helianthemum guttatum f. patulum
intro: Helianthemum guttatum f. patulum, also known as rockrose, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Cistaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Helianthemum guttatum f. patulum include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of rockrose include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and infections.
